title: Goeppertia straminea
taxonomy: Goeppertia straminea is classified within the family Marantaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the prayer plant family. This family is part of the order Zingiberales, which is a larger group of flowering plants that also includes ginger and bananas. Goeppertia straminea is further classified as a genus within the family Marantaceae, alongside other similar species. The genus Goeppertia is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Goeppertia straminea provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationship to other plant species.
uses: Goeppertia straminea, also known as Stramineus Calathea, has been traditionally used in medicine.
